At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

5180005009270 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県丹羽郡扶桑町斎藤宮添3', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:前利神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397050}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Sakito Shrine | 画像=[[File:前利神社（2024年2月）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Saitō Miyasoe 3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} | 社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Sakito Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sakito Shrine''' (さきとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its main sanctuary is said to stand atop a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said to be a shrine where the local powerful clan Sakito no Muraji (Niwa no Omi) enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records “Senior Third Rank Upper Sakito Tenjin.” Former Saitō Village is written as Sakito in the ''Wamyōshō,'' and also as Sakito Village in the {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13) {{ill|lt=land survey register|Q11540912|WD=Q11540912|1=kenchichō}}. Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. Place names “Agata-yashiki” and “Agata-yū” remain in the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さきとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

Its main sanctuary is said to stand atop a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said to be a shrine where the local powerful clan Sakito no Muraji (Niwa no Omi) enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records “Senior Third Rank Upper Sakito Tenjin.” Former Saitō Village is written as Sakito in the ''Wamyōshō,'' and also as Sakito Village in the {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13) {{ill|lt=land survey register|Q11540912|WD=Q11540912|1=kenchichō}}. Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. Place names “Agata-yashiki” and “Agata-yū” remain in the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|WD=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 5180005009270 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県丹羽郡扶桑町斎藤宮添3', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:前利神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11397050}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Sakito Shrine | 画像=[[File:前利神社（2024年2月）.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Saitō Miyasoe 3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} | 社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Sakito Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sakito Shrine''' (さきとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its main sanctuary is said to stand atop a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said to be a shrine where the local powerful clan Sakito no Muraji (Niwa no Omi) enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records “Senior Third Rank Upper Sakito Tenjin.” Former Saitō Village is written as Sakito in the ''Wamyōshō,'' and also as Sakito Village in the {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13) {{ill|lt=land survey register|Q11540912|WD=Q11540912|1=kenchichō}}. Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. Place names “Agata-yashiki” and “Agata-yū” remain in the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]][[Category:qq]]

A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Saitō Miyasoe 3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} | 社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Sakito Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sakito Shrine''' (さきとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its main sanctuary is said to stand atop a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said to be a shrine where the local powerful clan Sakito no Muraji (Niwa no Omi) enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records “Senior Third Rank Upper Sakito Tenjin.” Former Saitō Village is written as Sakito in the ''Wamyōshō,'' and also as Sakito Village in the {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13) {{ill|lt=land survey register|Q11540912|WD=Q11540912|1=kenchichō}}. Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. Place names “Agata-yashiki” and “Agata-yū” remain in the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]]

Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}{{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}} Saitō Miyasoe 3 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} | 社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭= | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Sakito Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sakito Shrine''' (さきとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Fusō|Q1203107|WD=Q1203107|1=Fusō, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|WD=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its main sanctuary is said to stand atop a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped kofun|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}. === History === Its founding period is unknown. It is said to be a shrine where the local powerful clan Sakito no Muraji (Niwa no Omi) enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}. The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records “Senior Third Rank Upper Sakito Tenjin.” Former Saitō Village is written as Sakito in the ''Wamyōshō,'' and also as Sakito Village in the {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|WD=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13) {{ill|lt=land survey register|Q11540912|WD=Q11540912|1=kenchichō}}. Also, the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}} has “in April, {{ill|lt=841|Q24870|WD=Q24870|1=841}} ({{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8), Sakito no Muraji were granted the surname Agata no Muraji, descendants of Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto...,” indicating this place was their base, and thus the shrine is taken as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Agatanushi Sakito no Muraji. Place names “Agata-yashiki” and “Agata-yū” remain in the area. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received devotion from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokugawa family|1=UNKNOWN}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}. In October {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}} personally visited, wrote “Sakito Shrine” on a {{ill|lt=hengaku|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} and dedicated it, showing its high prestige''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.506. It is also said to have been built atop a kofun of a powerful clan related to the Sakito lineage. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5) it became gōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10) was renamed as gōsha status notation. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) the shrine buildings were newly built, and in {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|WD=Q18645|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13) it became prefectural shrine. In early morning April 1, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|WD=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), arson burned down about 400 m² of the main shrine building''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Feb 23, 2017, suburban edition p.22, “Sakito Shrine arson suspicion; Inuyama police re-arrest restaurant worker”. It was rebuilt on October 30, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|WD=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|WD=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1). New shrine buildings are about 7 m² for the main sanctuary and 40 m² for the worship hall. Reconstruction cost about 110 million yen, funded by over 50 million yen donated by 1,107 local people plus insurance money''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 16, 2019, Owari edition p.10, “New shrine building completed at Fusō Sakito Shrine after overcoming fire”. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} (かみやいみみのみこと) === Site of Kōzuka === At Agata-yū in Saitō there were two mounds, Kashi-zuka and Tsubaki-zuka. These are said to have been inside the residence of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu in ancient times, and in the medieval period enshrined Shagū-shin and Fukuno-miya connected to Agata no Muraji. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, they were moved into Sakito Shrine precincts and are now {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}}. These mounds are said to possibly be kofun of relatives of Agatanushi no Muraji Ujimasu''Fusō Town History (Vol.2)'' p.507. === Other === It is said to be the origin of the name of {{ill|lt=Saitō (Fusō)|Q108114794|WD=Q108114794|lt=Saitō|1=Saito, Fuso}}, an {{ill|lt=ōaza|Q424857|WD=Q424857|1=ōaza}} of Fusō Town''Chūnichi Shimbun'', Dec 28, 2019, suburban edition p.16, “Fusō Newspaper: Place-name History, Saitō - calming divine anger”. In ancient times Saitō was called Sakito or Sakito (前刀). It is unclear when it changed to Saitō, but it is said to have been called Saitō in the Edo period''Fusō Town History (Vol.1)''. A tradition also says that when epidemics spread in the Edo period, because the shrine and village had the same name people thought it was divine anger and changed 前利 to 斎藤. It was also called “Sakito shrine” until somewhat later. === Location === * Saitō Miyasoe 3, Fusō, Niwa District, Aichi Prefecture ** About 500 m northwest of {{ill|lt=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School|Q48756647|WD=Q48756647|1=Fusō Municipal Fusō Junior High School}}. === Cultural property === * Hanging scroll of Sakito Shrine: calligraphy by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|Q3530497|WD=Q3530497|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, Fusō Town designated tangible cultural property (calligraphy)[https://www.town.fuso.lg.jp/gakushu/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/yuke/sakitojinja.html Tangible cultural property: Sakito Shrine hanging scroll] Fusō Town. Accessed Aug 1, 2021. === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Fusō Station|Q5510430|WD=Q5510430|1=Fusō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|WD=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} of {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|WD=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakito Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:斎藤]] [[Category:扶桑町の歴史]] [[Category:扶桑町の建築物]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]]
